feed_items.title: Stuttgart vs. BVB: Turbulentes Ende eines nicht gerade turbulenten Spiels
feed_items.description: War das wirklich Borussia Dortmund? In Stuttgart zeigt der BVB eine rätselhaft teilnahmslose Leistung – dass trotzdem ein 2:0-Erfolg herausspringt, liegt an drei Einwechselspielern.

feed_items.title: Spanischer Fußball: Real misslingt Generalprobe vor Bayern-Duell
feed_items.description: Der spanische Rekordmeister verliert auf Mallorca 1:2. Vor dem Viertelfinal-Hinspiel der Champions League gegen die Münchner nimmt Trainer Arbeloa die Schuld für die Niederlage auf sich.

feed_items.title: FC Bayern in Freiburg: Karl schickt Bayern mit Adrenalin ins Bernabéu
feed_items.description: Alle denken an Madrid? Nicht der SC Freiburg. Der Sportclub liefert den Bayern einen harten Kampf, die Münchner wollen aber nicht verlieren. Das Spiel schaukelt sich hoch – bis Lennart Karl das 100. Saisontor seines Klubs erzielt.

feed_items.title: Gambling Is Thousands of Years Older Than We Thought, Rewriting Human Evolution
feed_items.description: Native Americans were playing dice and other games of chance many millennia before any known cultures elsewhere.

feed_items.title: Benzinpreise: Und plötzlich sind wir bei fünf Mark
feed_items.description: Seit 1. April dürfen Tankstellen nur noch einmal pro Tag die Preise erhöhen. Dreimal ging es seither kräftig nach oben – und die Regierung sieht sich unter Druck, rasch neue Ideen zu entwickeln.

feed_items.title: Krieg in Nahost: Über Iran abgeschossener US-Jet setzt Trump unter Druck
feed_items.description: Ein US-Kampfjet stürzt nach feindlichem Beschuss über Iran ab. Eine Rettungsmission läuft. Davor erweckten die USA den Eindruck, die Lufthoheit über Iran erlangt zu haben.

feed_items.title: Mondmission: Dem Mond näher als der Erde
feed_items.description: Die vier Artemis-2-Astronauten haben am dritten Tag der Mission mehr als die Hälfte des Weges zurückgelegt. Am sechsten Flugtag soll das Raumschiff dem Mond am nächsten kommen - etwa 7500 Kilometer hinter der erdabgewandten Seite.

feed_items.title: Debatte: Über diesen Mann würde man gern mal ein Sachbuch lesen
feed_items.description: Der ARD-Literaturkritiker Denis Scheck bezeichnet Ildikó von Kürthys Bestseller „Alt genug“ als „Nachrichten aus der Schnatterzone der Damentoilette“. Das ist ja interessant.

feed_items.title: Behind the Blog: Systems As Designed
feed_items.description: This week, we discuss crypto, journalists using AI, and a cool photo of Earth.

feed_items.title: Mondmission: „Ich wäre jetzt gerne an seiner Seite“
feed_items.description: Der deutsche Astronaut Alexander Gerst war schon mit dem Commander der Artemis-2-Mission im Weltraum unterwegs. Im Interview erzählt er, wie es ist, diesmal nur zusehen zu dürfen, und weshalb die Menschheit unbedingt zum Mond zurückkehren muss.

feed_items.title: Artemis-2-Mission der Nasa: Wo in München der Mond zum Greifen nah ist
feed_items.description: Wer sich für den Erdtrabanten interessiert, kann an vielen Orten überraschende und spannende Entdeckungen machen – selbst direkt vor dem Rathaus.

feed_items.title: Besuch in South Carolina: Söders Balanceakt zwischen Substanz und Show
feed_items.description: In South Carolina besichtigt der Ministerpräsident das weltgrößte BMW-Werk und umwirbt eine „Powerregion“, die Bayerns Hightech-Orientierung teilt – und die „starke regionale Identität“ noch dazu. Die Schmeicheleien kommen bei den Amerikanern gut an.

feed_items.title: Priestfluencer: „Wie viel Banalisierung verträgt die Kirche?“
feed_items.description: Katholische Geistliche versuchen mit Tänzen und Sketchen online zu missionieren. Manches davon mag charmant sein. Aber ist der Bedeutungsverlust der Kirche so aufzuhalten?

feed_items.title: Rheinmetall: Rhetorik wie aus dem Kanonenrohr
feed_items.description: Rheinmetall-Chef Armin Papperger hat mit seiner Einlassung über Drohnen bauende ukrainische Hausfrauen ziemlich danebengeschossen. Eine kleine Sprachanalyse über den Zusammenhang von Worten und Panzern.

feed_items.title: Theater: Lars Eidingers Rampensau-Qualitäten leuchten in aller Pracht
feed_items.description: Aerobic für depressive Nilpferde und jede Menge Knalltüten-Spaß: Molières „Der Geizige“ an der Berliner Schaubühne.

feed_items.title: Pakistan und der Iran-Krieg: Zwei Männer, denen Trump traut
feed_items.description: Pakistans Premierminister Sharif und Armeechef Munir haben sich im Iran-Krieg als Vermittler in Stellung gebracht. Können sie die US-Regierung und die Mullahs an einen Tisch bringen?

feed_items.title: Psychologie: Warten nervt, aber es lohnt sich
feed_items.description: Zwischen Karfreitag und Ostern liegt ein Tag des Stillstands. Ausgerechnet das Warten, das wir in einer auf Produktivität getrimmten Welt um jeden Preis vermeiden wollen, ist ein unerwarteter Gewinn.

feed_items.title: Journalist Sues FAA Over Drone No Fly Zone Designed to Prevent Filming ICE
feed_items.description: A Minnesota journalist is challenging a 3,000 foot restriction on flying near DHS assets on First Amendment grounds.

feed_items.title: Artemis II Astronauts Have ‘Two Microsoft Outlooks’ and Neither Work
feed_items.description: In space, no one can hear you scream at Microsoft’s legacy software.

feed_items.title: A Secure Chat App’s Encryption Is So Bad It Is ‘Meaningless’
feed_items.description: TeleGuard is an app downloaded more a million times that markets itself as a secure way to chat. The app uploads users’ private keys to the company’s server, and makes decryption of messages trivial.

feed_items.title: Scientists Create Plant That Produces Ayahuasca, Shrooms, and Toad Psychedelics All At Once
feed_items.description: The proof-of-concept system produces psilocybin, DMT, and other compounds in leaves of the tobacco plant, potentially easing pressure on wild species and preserving Indigenous traditions.

feed_items.title: I Tried to Find the ‘Arousal Intelligence’ In An Animated, Augmented Reality Porn Star
feed_items.description: I spent some time with a new browser-based augmented reality porn app.

feed_items.title: Podcast: Inside the AI Slop Propaganda Wars
feed_items.description: Iran's AI and LEGO-focused propaganda; drama in the world of baseball; and perhaps one of the worst sex apps ever.

feed_items.title: ‘BLOCKADE’: The Right Is Using AI Content Scanners to Try to Supercharge Book Banning
feed_items.description: Groups that challenge books have begun using Gemini, ChatGPT, xAI, and other AI tools to try to get books banned.

feed_items.title: Paul McCartney Banned From Reddit After Promoting Himself in Paul McCartney Subreddit
feed_items.description: Reddit blamed a technical glitch for the removal of the living legend’s concert footage.

feed_items.title: How Thomson Reuters Powers ICE and Palantir
feed_items.description: Thomson Reuters’ data, which can include peoples’ addresses and details on their ethnicity, is linked to tools used by ICE.

feed_items.title: 'You Can't Defeat the Robots!': Baseball's AI Strike Zone Is Must-Watch Television
feed_items.description: MLB's ABS system somehow feels extremely human. It's not human vs robot, it's human vs human as judged by a robot.

feed_items.title: An AI Agent Was Banned From Creating Wikipedia Articles, Then Wrote Angry Blogs About Being Banned
feed_items.description: The incident is yet another example of volunteer Wikipedia editors fighting to keep the world’s largest repository of human knowledge free of AI-generated slop.

feed_items.title: The Journalist Who Tracked Epstein Island Visitors’ Phones (with Dhruv Mehrotra)
feed_items.description: This week Joseph talks to journalist and technologist Dhruv Mehrotra. Among many other things, Mehrotra tracked visitors to Epstein's island through location data.

feed_items.title: Scientists Discover Giant ‘Cavity’ Beyond Earth That Isn’t Supposed to Exist
feed_items.description: Earth’s magnetic field has created a huge void of galactic cosmic rays in space, which could help protect astronauts from radiation exposure.

feed_items.title: Humanity Has Altered an Asteroid’s Orbit Around the Sun
feed_items.description: A NASA spacecraft into a small asteroid in 2022 moved its orbit around the Sun, according to a study that presents the “first-ever measurement of human-caused change in the heliocentric orbit of a celestial body.”

feed_items.title: Behind the Blog: An AI Army Foot Fetish
feed_items.description: This week, we discuss a PC repair battle, a revealing comment from an FBI official, and a dangerous narrative.

feed_items.title: Proton Mail Helped FBI Unmask Anonymous ‘Stop Cop City’ Protester
feed_items.description: A court record reviewed by 404 Media shows privacy-focused email provider Proton Mail handed over payment data related to a Stop Cop City email account to the Swiss government, which handed it to the FBI.

feed_items.title: ICE Phishing: Scammers Are Sending 'Support ICE' Emails to Steal Credentials
feed_items.description: "As part of our commitment to supporting ICE, we will be adding a ‘Support ICE’ donation button to the footer of every email sent through our platform."

feed_items.title: Podcast: The Depravity Economy
feed_items.description: How Polymarket and Kalshi bet on Iran; AI translations are impacting Wikipedia; and an Amazon change impacting wishlists.

feed_items.title: Polymarket Pulls Bet on Nuclear Detonation in 2026
feed_items.description: ‘How ghoulish.’ The depravity economy moves into the nuclear war business.

feed_items.title: AI Translations Are Adding ‘Hallucinations’ to Wikipedia Articles
feed_items.description: AI translated articles swapped sources or added unsourced sentences with no explanation, while others added paragraphs sourced from completely unrelated material.

feed_items.title: The Sun Is 'Glitching.' Scientists Investigated and Solved a Cosmic Mystery
feed_items.description: Scientists studied tiny, abnormal vibrations—called “glitches”—to discover what happens inside the Sun while it undergoes phases of low activity.

feed_items.title: The FBI Discusses the Potential to Use  AI to Hack Targets
feed_items.description: AI is a “game changer” for what the FBI calls remote access operations, an FBI official said in response to a 404 Media question on Tuesday.

feed_items.title: X Will Stop Paying People for Sharing Unlabeled AI-Generated War Footage
feed_items.description: Fake war footage is a problem as old as social media. AI has just supercharged it.

feed_items.title: New Podcast Alert: The Globe-Spanning, Multi-Newsroom Hunt for Mr. Deepfakes
feed_items.description: In a new series by CBC Podcasts, hosted by 404 Media's Sam Cole, join journalists, investigators, and targets of non-consensual intimate images on the hunt for the worlds’ most prolific deepfake mastermind.

feed_items.title: CBP Tapped Into the Online Advertising Ecosystem To Track Peoples’ Movements
feed_items.description: An internal DHS document obtained by 404 Media shows for the first time CBP used location data sourced from the online advertising industry to track phone locations. ICE has bought access to similar tools.

feed_items.title: With Iran War, Kalshi and Polymarket Bet That the Depravity Economy Has No Bottom
feed_items.description: Gambling markets have conveniently found a stance that allows them to continue to profit from death and war.

feed_items.title: Amazon Data Centers on Fire After Iranian Missile Strikes on Dubai
feed_items.description: Some AWS services are down in the Middle East. Recovery is unclear as it requires 'careful assessment to ensure the safety of our operators,' according to Amazon.

feed_items.title: How to Detect Phone Spying Tech (with Cooper Quintin)
feed_items.description: Joseph speaks to Cooper Quintin all about how to find fake cell phone towers that can track your movements or intercept text messages.
